article_meta.title: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-NY) called the SAVE America Act “one of the most despicable pieces of legislatio

reasoning: Mixed performance: correctly flags several author-level loaded phrases but uses non-approved bias labels, includes one attribution error by flagging a sentence containing quoted text, and misses at least one notable opinion-as-fact/causal claim in the author’s prose.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: One violation: the fourth item’s biasedText includes quoted language, breaching the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Three items are solid author-prose bias (e.g., 'rant', 'dragging his feet', 'Democrat leader'). The fourth is poorly framed (includes a quote and miscasts omission), making it questionable.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed a significant author-level claim: 'the act is likely to fail due to Thune’s unwillingness to amend the filibuster' (opinion/prediction stated as fact without attribution).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All four labels are outside the allowed taxonomy and at least one is misapplied; appropriate labels would be Word Choice/Spin/Slant, not 'Loaded Language/Framing/Labeling/Selection-Omission'.
